details.restore_scope: Restore the timestamp-matched Compose override and Mongo base-config backup, then recreate only LibreChat-API and verify the root, API config, CodeAPI health, GPT/Fable chat, and one canonical code-tool call.

details.description: Production LibreChat after the Fable legacy alias release and before the public code tool contract

checks: Public contract is empty when no code/file tools are registered
Contract lists only canonical tools actually registered for the run in stable order
execute_code is identified as a capability marker and is not normalized
Existing Bash, Read, and Skill compatibility tests remain passing
Mongo GPT/Fable prompt cleanup preserves unrelated fields and is idempotent
Mongo preflight accepts both a legacy GPT tool paragraph and a current GPT prompt with no model-specific tool paragraph
node --check passed for api-index.cjs, code-tool-contract.cjs, tool-call-normalizer.cjs, and mongo-config.js
bash -n passed for deploy.sh and remote-apply.sh
candidate api-index.cjs differs from the deployed baseline only at the public contract import and append point

change_summary.expected_result: Every current or future model using the LibreChat Agent code/file path receives one runtime contract generated from the tools actually registered for that run, so model-specific prompts no longer drift toward Bash, execute_code, or unavailable Claude Code CLI names.
functions: Build a stable public contract from the current run's registered bash_tool, read_file, skill, create_file, and edit_file definitions
Append the contract once in the common Agent initializer after code, file-authoring, and Skill registration
Identify execute_code as a capability marker rather than a callable tool
Retain the strict Bash, Read, and Skill compatibility normalizer in the common ON_TOOL_EXECUTE entry
Remove conflicting code-tool-name sentences from the current GPT and Fable model prompts while preserving Office and file isolation rules
limitations: The public contract applies to the LibreChat Agent execution path; a future non-Agent integration requires its own acceptance
Grep, Glob, Edit, LS, execute_code, and unknown names remain unsupported and are not converted
The contract lists only tools actually registered for the current run and does not enable capabilities by itself
change_summary.reason: The Fable incident proved that per-model tool-name prompts drift from the Agent registry. GPT had previously described Bash or execute_code, while the current Admin configuration later replaced that whole paragraph with unrelated safety rules. Both states show that model-specific text is not a durable source of tool truth.
change_summary.risk: Enhanced common Agent-instruction and base-config change. An incorrect contract could advertise an unavailable tool to every code-capable model. The implementation derives names from actual tool definitions, emits nothing when no relevant tool is registered, preserves strict alias boundaries, uses immutable API mounts, and includes Compose and Mongo rollback backups.

details.command: Restore the timestamp-matched /opt/librechat/compose.override.yaml.bak-* and public-code-tool-contract-* Mongo backup created by remote-apply.sh, recreate only LibreChat-API, then verify /, /api/config, CodeAPI health, GPT/Fable normal chat, and one code-tool call.

details.api_error_log_check: No legacy tool-not-found, uncaught, unhandled, fatal, syntax, type, or reference errors in the acceptance window.

validation_plan.command: node scripts/test-release.js and node scripts/test-mongo-config.js in the versioned patch directory
validation_plan.expected: The contract is empty without registered code/file tools, lists only actual canonical tools in stable order, keeps strict alias behavior, and the Mongo migration preserves unrelated configuration and is idempotent.

validation_plan.command: node --check, bash -n, git diff --check, and scripts/validate-release-governance.sh
validation_plan.expected: Candidate runtime modules, deployment scripts, repository formatting, and all release-governance tests pass.

validation_plan.command: Authenticated vip998 code-tool acceptance with at most one billable model request
validation_plan.expected: A code-capable model calls a canonical registered tool directly, the response completes, the runtime contract names match the tool registry, and the acceptance window has no Tool execute_code/Bash/Read/Skill not found error.
